title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model capable of 1,000+ tokens per second on standard hardware using selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime. Better Stack stress-tested the model with real-world programming tasks and found it capable of high throughput but with quality degradation under extreme load.
writeup: Better Stack stress-tests MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model from Xiaomi and TileRT that reaches 1,000-plus tokens per second on standard hardware. The throughput comes from a stack of techniques: selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime. The tests use real-world programming tasks, and the takeaway is mixed: the model sustains high throughput but shows quality degradation under extreme load. The result matters as a concrete data point on how far MoE inference optimizations push local serving speed before output suffers.
